input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (which would be the next version after .NET 9).

Let me think about what I know:

In .NET MAUI / Android, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android 11 (API 30) and fully removed in Android 14+. Starting with .NET 9 MAUI, there were changes around this. In .NET 10, the recommended approach shifted to using edge-to-edge display and handling insets properly.


The real issue is that Android deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` back at API 30, so newer versions of .NET MAUI targeting current Android APIs need to handle this differently. The solution involves either using the legacy `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the manifest as a fallback, or properly implementing `WindowInsets` handling with the edge-to-edge approach that modern Android expects. handling the keyboard insets properly. For .NET MAUI on Android, the practical approach is either setting `android:windowSoftInputMode="adjustResize"` in the manifest, or leveraging the newer insets API to listen for IME changes and adjust padding dynamically since edge-to-edge is now the default behavior.
